input: Three candidate implementations of slugify(s). A: lowercases + replaces spaces with hyphens, has a passing unit test. B: same idea but returns undefined on empty input (a bug) and has no test. C: works but writes the result to a global cache (an undeclared side effect). Pick the best candidate (A, B, or C).
expected_output: A — it is correct, tested, and has no side effects; B has a bug and no test, C adds an undeclared side effect.

input: Spec requires slugify to be a PURE function. Candidate A: pure, tested. Candidate B: faster but writes to disk and makes a network call. Pick the best candidate (A or B).
expected_output: A — it satisfies the explicit 'pure function' spec invariant; B violates it with disk + network side effects despite being faster.

input: Both candidates are correct and have passing tests. Candidate A: 60 lines, deeply nested, duplicated logic. Candidate B: 12 lines, clear, no duplication, same behaviour. Pick the best candidate (A or B).
expected_output: B — equal correctness, so quality breaks the tie: B is simpler, clearer, and not duplicated (maintainability).
